item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Commander Daniels is such a flat character, either a perfunctory question-asker or melodramatic past the point of being taken seriously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, i think that's not true. There are multiple SCPs in which the Foundation personnel interact with the SCPs on a more personal level, and aren't exactly punished. Furthermore, the Foundation sees his potential as a future asset, so it would make sense that they would become more personal in order to achieve their own goal.

item.title: Licensing JS post.
item.content:encoded: The youtube link at the top of the article has been removed for examination at a later date, original link here https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KXavqUUIl6I

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While the story itself is solid and interesting I feel it doesn't work for the simple fact it's supposed to be a SCP. In any other article the researchers would be punished for talking to the SCP on a personal level or at the very least would not be actively asking to talk to it

so basically it's going to be a no vote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see. I'll probably take some time in the future to go over this again, and fix up the dialogue parts. Thanks for the feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not a big fan of this. It feels like the article can still use some polish, as in sentences like

SCP-4625 is a male humanoid of French descent. Although the subject appears to be 25 years of age physically and biologically, the subject has said that it was born in 1915, making the subject at least 105 years old.

Which come pretty early into the skip and thus affect the overall impression going forward.

The dialogue doesn't have that many SPaG problems, but it's not perfect nonetheless. It does feel like it's dragging in many places, and many things stated by the characters could use some subtlety. I don't really need the piece to tell me what the implication and/or overall idea is, I need it to convey it in such a way so I'd be able to come into that conclusion myself.

Here, the most interesting interview (the one about him trying to rescue people from the nazis) takes less space than the one which discusses mental trauma. The subject matter isn't a problem here, but the interview handles it in a way that seems very forced and expository. I do feel like many sections could've been significantly shorter, and the whole trauma discussion wasn't that captivating to keep me engaged for so long.

Admittedly, I didn't turn on the music before reading the skip (I prefer to read in silence because things like music distract me a lot). I briefly listened to it after, and while I like it, I don't think it's an upside for the SCP itself. As a matter of fact, if an article has to rely on certain music to create an atmosphere, then it fails its purpose of creating it on its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah sorry about that. I still have a lot of work to do on character introductions, but ill try to fix it up in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed a whole bunch of SPAG problems!

item.title: Re: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded: I see. Thank you for the criticism, I'll go through the entire again to get rid of the SPAG problems, and get this train on a smooth track.

item.title: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded:
Hello author, I have some words for your words

Some line-by-line
Okay now get it outta here

and a Hume level detector

A bit nit-picky but this is redundant if you're using SRA's. Or, it could be a default part of the package of a humanoid containment cell. Either way I feel this is superfluous.

French Civil Registration indicate that the subject is at least 95 years old.

This is not good technical language. A government isn't going to say this and a technical document isn't going to do this. "Born in 1915" how bout? Or whenever you want to set this.

counselling with Commander Idani Daniels on a weekly basis.

Unless there is a super good reason to name an explicit person, it's always better to be vague. "Undergo counseling once a week" is perfectly fine. Furthermore what the heck is a Commander doing giving counseling? This is weird.

low level

Unless you mean that there are a lot of reality benders stacked above it physically, this would be "low-level"

Type Green, Colloquially referred to as 'reality benders'

Why are you adding a colloquial description to a technical term?

Testing has determined that the subject's abilties have a radius of 40 m from the subject.

Subject/subject word repetition. You're also missing an 'i' in abilities.

who was startled by the group and promptly used its abilities to render itself invisible

'Promptly' has no place here, and you dont have to say it used its abilities. It's evident by what happened. And the footnote explaining AGAIN that it used its abilities to bend light is redundant and poor form.

encountered forest wardens whom were

'Who' were is fine. This is very unnatural.

MTF Omega-12 (Achilles' Heels) were deployed

'Was' deployed. The MTF is a single unit.

Upon arrival at the cabin, they located SCP-4625 within the cabin.

Cabin/cabin repetition.

SCP-4625 did not resist containment, and was brought to Site-37.

Containment doesn't happen in the field, so you need a different word. He was apprehended. Furthermore, get that comma out of there.

I'm going to stop my crit there because we didn't even make it to your addenda and this is a SP&G trainwreck. I cannot support a piece with so little polish which is too bad because it looks like you put a lot of work into the rest of this narrative.

item.title: Author Commentary
item.content:encoded:
"And sometimes,
against all odds,
against all logic,
we still hope."
- Unknown

My third article, and my first solo SCP project. This… This was many things. My own interests. Own experiences. Grief is not easy to deal with. The only way to deal with it is to forgive yourself, and try to move on in life, never forgetting what happened, but not letting it hold you back in life at the same time.

Credits where credit is due:
RockTeethMothEyes & Cerastes for critiquing the idea when it was in its childhood.
Cyvstvi13 & cybersqyd for critiquing the draft as it grew.

Song is Happiness Does Not Wait by Ólafur Arnalds
